settingsSync.toggleDesc: Stores this vault's settings (daily notes, templates, task database, backup rules and more) as a small file inside the vault so they match on all your devices. Requires an active cloud sync.
settingsSync.explainer: No credentials and no device-specific paths are transferred. If you change settings on more than one device, the most recently saved version wins.
settingsSync.secretsDesc: Only static IMAP/CalDAV passwords and custom Google Calendar app credentials; OAuth tokens stay on this device.

encryption.contentRowDesc: Store this vault's files as ciphertext on the sync server. Your local copy always stays plain Markdown.
encryption.activateConfirm: Every file will be re-uploaded to the sync server as ciphertext. Your local vault stays plain Markdown and is never at risk. Test this on a throwaway vault first. Continue?
encryption.activateStarted: Encryption started — {{n}} files are being re-uploaded as ciphertext.
encryption.completeConfirm: Switch to strict mode: from now on only ciphertext is accepted. Do this only once every file has finished syncing.
encryption.completeDone: Migration finished — the connection now accepts only ciphertext.
encryption.contentActiveNote: End-to-end encryption is active. New writes are uploaded as ciphertext; the local vault stays plain Markdown.
encryption.contentWarning: When you turn this on, every file is re-uploaded to the sync server as ciphertext. The local vault always stays plain Markdown.
encryption.statusNone: No sync passphrase set. Set one to sync account credentials encrypted between your devices.
encryption.statusLocked: Encryption is set up but locked on this device. Enter the passphrase to unlock it.
encryption.statusUnlocked: Encryption is active — synced settings travel encrypted.
encryption.scopeNote: This encrypts the app settings and account credentials that sync between your devices — not the vault’s notes and files. To encrypt the vault content, use Security & Sharing.
encryption.passphraseRowDesc: Protects the encrypted settings and account credentials that travel with this vault.
encryption.everyStart: Ask for the passphrase on every start
encryption.everyStartDesc: Don't keep the key on this device; re-enter the passphrase after each restart.
encryption.recoveryBody: Keep this code somewhere safe. It is the only way to recover encrypted data without your passphrase and without a second device.
encryption.tooShort: The passphrase is too short (at least {{min}} characters).
encryption.deactivateConfirm: Every remote file will be rewritten as plaintext. Your local vault is unchanged. Continue?
encryption.deactivateStarted: Decryption started — {{n}} files are being rewritten.
encryption.deactivateDone: Encryption is off; the authenticated plaintext state is active.
encryption.rotateConfirm: Enter the sync passphrase. A new key will be created and every remote file will be re-encrypted.
encryption.rotateStarted: Key rotation started — {{n}} files are being re-encrypted.
encryption.rotateFailed: Could not start key rotation. Check the passphrase.
encryption.rotateDone: Key rotation finished; the old key was removed.
encryption.migrationActive: A resumable encryption migration is active. Finish it after the sync queue is empty.

contextMenu.label: Context menu
contextMenu.copyFailed: Could not copy to the clipboard.
contextMenu.pasteFailed: Could not paste from the clipboard.

settings.askBeforeCreateLinkDesc: Clicking a link to a note that doesn't exist yet normally creates it right away. With this option, you're asked first.
settings.title: Settings
settings.dailyNotesTemplate: Daily Note Template (Filename in Template Folder)
settings.driveByoDesc: Bring Your Own Credentials: Create a project in the Google Cloud Console, enable the Google Drive API, and create a Desktop App OAuth client under 'Credentials'.
settings.driveFolderDesc: Plainva creates this folder in your Drive (default 'Plainva') and synchronizes its entire content – including externally added files. In testing mode of the Google App, the login expires after 7 days and must be renewed.
settings.driveAuthProgress: Login in progress - please confirm in your browser...
settings.driveConnected: Authorized. Plainva is syncing via Google Drive.
settings.driveAuthHint: 'Connect to Google' opens the Google login in your browser.
settings.oneDriveDesc: Syncs this vault with a folder in your OneDrive (Microsoft Graph). No client secret is needed; sign-in runs through your browser.
settings.oneDriveClientIdDesc: Application (client) ID of an Entra app registration of type 'Mobile and desktop applications' with redirect URI http://localhost. Pre-filled once Plainva ships its own registration.
settings.oneDriveFolderDesc: Plainva creates this folder in your OneDrive (default 'Plainva') and synchronizes its entire content – including externally added files.
settings.oneDriveAuthProgress: Login in progress - please confirm in your browser...
settings.oneDriveConnected: Authorized. Plainva is syncing via OneDrive.
settings.oneDriveAuthHint: 'Connect to Microsoft' opens the Microsoft login in your browser.
settings.dropboxDesc: Syncs this vault with a folder in your Dropbox. Requires full-Dropbox access so existing folders can be used; sign-in runs through your browser.
settings.dropboxAppKeyDesc: App key of a Dropbox app with full-Dropbox access and redirect URI http://127.0.0.1:41953. Pre-filled once Plainva ships its own app.
settings.dropboxRootPathDesc: Absolute folder in your Dropbox that holds the vault (default '/Plainva'). Created if it does not exist.
settings.dropboxAuthProgress: Login in progress - please confirm in your browser...
settings.dropboxConnected: Authorized. Plainva is syncing via Dropbox.
settings.dropboxAuthHint: 'Connect to Dropbox' opens the Dropbox login in your browser.
settings.s3Desc: Syncs this vault with an S3-compatible bucket (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, Backblaze B2, MinIO, Wasabi, …). Key-based – no browser login.
settings.s3EndpointDesc: Base URL of the S3 API, e.g. https://s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com, https://<account>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com or http://127.0.0.1:9000 for MinIO.
settings.s3RegionDesc: SigV4 region. 'us-east-1' works for most non-AWS stores; Cloudflare R2 uses 'auto'.
settings.s3PrefixDesc: Subfolder inside the bucket the vault lives under. Empty = bucket root.
settings.s3PathStyleDesc: Recommended (works for MinIO, R2 and most compatibles). Disable for virtual-hosted AWS buckets (bucket.host URLs).
settings.syncIntervalDesc: How often this vault is checked for remote changes (min. {{min}} s). Local saves are uploaded immediately. Changes are applied automatically.
settings.providerSaveHint: Plainva deliberately applies sync credentials only via “Save” or “Connect” — they go to the system keychain and replace the credentials of other providers.
settings.autoOpenLastVaultDesc: Unchecked, Plainva always starts on the vault chooser screen.
settings.vaultNotOpenHint: This vault is not open. Changes will be saved and active next time you open it.
settings.defaultNoteTypeDesc: Written into the frontmatter of every newly created note (OKF required field).
settings.dailyNoteTypeDesc: type value for newly created daily notes; a template with its own type wins.
settings.okfConversionDesc: {{count}} file(s) do not conform to the OKF format (missing type field or reserved names).
settings.okfIndexDesc: Generate directory listings per folder or adopt existing overview notes.
settings.backupZipEnabledDesc: Creates a ZIP of the whole vault (without .plainva) once a day in the background and keeps the most recent backups.
settings.backupZipKeepDesc: Older ZIP backups are deleted automatically.
settings.versionIntervalDesc: How often at most a new file version is created while editing. Deleting always snapshots.
settings.versionMaxCountDesc: Above this count the oldest versions of a file are removed.
settings.versionMaxAgeDesc: Versions older than this are removed permanently (daily cleanup run).
settings.deletedFilesDesc: Lists snapshots of deleted files and restores them to their original location.
settings.updateErrorNoRelease: No public updates (releases) are available yet.
settings.activeSyncHint: still connected – use Disconnect below to stop syncing
settings.showCompatWarning: Show compatibility warnings for Plainva extensions
settings.dailyNotesFormatDesc: Dots and slashes are automatically replaced with hyphens.
settings.allowExtendedDbDesc: Enables advanced features that are not fully supported natively in Obsidian.
settings.densityDesc: Compact tightens lists, menus and table rows; note content is unaffected.
settings.defaultViewModeDesc: Notes open in this view; switching the mode applies to that file for the current session.
settings.autoUpdateCheckDesc: Quietly checks for new versions shortly after start and shows a notice when one is found.
settings.reportIssueDesc: Opens a new GitHub issue with prefilled version details (no vault content).
settings.contentFontSizeDesc: Font size of the editor and reading view; the interface itself is unaffected.
settings.contentFontFamilyDesc: Font of the note content. “Theme default” follows the selected theme.
settings.uiZoomDesc: Scales the entire interface. Also via Ctrl/Cmd + Plus/Minus; 0 resets.
settings.perfMetricsDesc: Local measurement points of this session (median/p95 in ms) — they never leave the device.
settings.perfMetricsEmpty: No measurements in this session yet.
settings.syncQueueDesc: Shows what is still waiting to reach the cloud (oldest first).
settings.rebuildIndexDesc: Rebuilds this vault's search index from scratch — helps when search, backlinks or databases look stale.
settings.taskDatabaseDesc: Tasks from the Tasks view can be promoted into this database.
settings.pageDescAppearance: Theme, mode, language and interface scaling.
settings.pageDescEditor: Default view and note content font.
settings.pageDescBehavior: What happens at startup and which hints appear.
settings.pageDescUpdates: Automatic checks and manual search for new versions.
settings.pageDescSync: Cloud provider and sync behavior of this vault.
settings.pageDescSecurity: Encryption, recovery, devices, and sharing for this vault.
settings.securityRecoveryChallenge: To confirm your backup, enter the two requested groups from the recovery code.
settings.pageDescPim: Calendar accounts, selection and default calendar for this vault.
settings.pageDescContent: Daily notes, templates, tasks and the OKF format.
settings.pageDescBackup: Automatic ZIP backups and the per-file version history.
settings.pageDescMaintenance: Search index, statistics and recovery for this vault.
settings.weekStartDesc: First day of the week in all calendar views.
settings.pageDescCloudAccounts: Connect this vault's providers and choose which services they carry.
settings.pageDescMail: Mailboxes, capture folder and privacy for this vault.

editor.noProperties: No properties found. Click the plus icon to add one.
editor.noActiveFileDesc: Select a note to view or edit.
editor.slashTemplateDesc: Insert a template's content at the caret
editor.slashFootnoteDesc: Reference in the text, definition at the end

editor.slashInternalImageDesc: Search & embed an image from the vault
editor.slashNewBaseDesc: Create a new .base in this folder & embed it
editor.slashDocIconDesc: Set an icon above the document
editor.slashHeaderColorDesc: Set the color stripe above the document
editor.fmtMultilineLink: Links can only be created within one line.
editor.blockFormatConflict: A line cannot be both a heading and a task. Use bold for a prominent task title.
editor.atNewBaseDesc: Create a new .base in the note's folder
editor.conflictBanner: Sync conflict: your version is stored in '{{path}}'. This file now shows the other side's state.
editor.conflictBannerNoPath: Sync conflict: your version was preserved as a conflict copy. This file now shows the other side's state.
editor.exportAttachmentsHint: Linked attachments (images etc.) are not exported — only the Markdown file.
editor.draftBanner: Found an unsaved draft (from {{time}}). Restore it?

sync.error: Sync error – click for details and retry
sync.massDeleteBody: Plainva has queued {{n}} of {{total}} synced files for deletion in the cloud without you confirming this deletion in Plainva — this typically happens when the local vault folder was emptied or moved outside the app. Do you really want these files deleted in the cloud as well?
sync.massDeleteRestored: {{n}} deletions discarded — the files will be restored from the cloud.
sync.massDeleteRestoreFailed: Could not discard the queued deletions.
sync.firstSyncBody: Plainva is syncing your notes with the cloud for the first time. Depending on how many there are, this can take a few minutes. You can keep working — the progress is shown in the status bar at the bottom right.
sync.none: Configure a sync provider (WebDAV, Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox or S3) for this vault to enable synchronization.
sync.errorHint: Common causes: a temporary network problem or a timeout — those usually clear up on their own, and Plainva retries automatically. Or the sign-in has expired or the credentials changed (e.g. with Google Drive) — then you can reconnect in the sync settings.
sync.recovered: The synchronization succeeded on the next attempt.
sync.retrying: Plainva is trying the synchronization again…
sync.authErrorHint: The sign-in expired or was revoked. Reconnect in the sync settings.
sync.transientErrorHint: This was probably a temporary network or provider problem. Plainva retries such errors automatically.
sync.resetEncryptionConfirm: Plainva will forget that this connection was encrypted and sync as plain text again. Only do this if the encrypted vault was removed on purpose. If the cloud still holds encrypted content, cancel here.
sync.encryptionErrorHint: This connection was known to be encrypted, but the encryption data is missing from the cloud (for example because the encrypted vault was deleted). Sync stops to protect your data. If you removed the vault on purpose, reset the encryption for this connection.

database.graphNoColumns: The graph shows no property columns.
database.confirmRenameProperty: Rename property "{{old}}" to "{{new}}" in {{count}} files. Continue?
database.embedScopeLabel: Filter this database to the current note
database.filterThisNoteTip: Filters to the current note when embedded; opened on its own, it shows all rows.
database.subItemsHint: Nested: entries with a parent relation appear collapsible under their parent entry in the table view. Off = flat list; the properties are kept.
database.noViews: No views configured in this database.
database.unsupportedView: Unsupported view type: {{type}}. Only 'table' is supported.
database.noDateField: No date field configured. Please choose a field in the configuration panel on the right.
database.missingStartDateDesc: This view ({{viewName}}) needs a start-date field.
database.missingFieldDesc: This view ({{viewName}}) needs a field of type
database.confirmDropTime: Switching to "Date only" will discard the time component of the selected fields. Continue?
database.confirmFillMissing: The property "{{column}}" will be added (empty) to {{count}} files. Continue?
database.noAssignedTemplates: No template assigned to this database yet
database.templateTargetsHint: This template appears in the “Entry” menu of its assigned databases. Without an assignment it only shows up there under “Show all templates”.
database.newItemFolderSetupHint: This database has no folder source yet. Choose where new items are stored — the folder is created and added as a source.
database.newItemFolderSetupTagHint: New items automatically receive this database's tag source. Choose the folder they are stored in.
database.newItemFolderChoiceHint: This database has several folder sources. Choose where new items are stored. The choice is saved in the database and can be changed anytime.
database.matchAll: All of the following conditions must match (AND):
database.matchAny: At least one of the following conditions must match (OR):
database.filterMatchAnyTip: At least one condition must match
database.complexFilterTip: Nested filter from Obsidian – it is kept and applied, but cannot be edited here.
database.wizardSourceHint: Which notes should this database show? At least one folder or one tag; combining them narrows the result.
database.wizardColumnsHint: Properties found in the matching notes – add them as columns?
database.missingFieldOptions: You can create a new field or convert an existing one.
database.pageDescView: The view type and display of this view.
database.pageDescColumns: Visibility and order in this view.
database.pageDescSort: The order of entries in this view.
database.pageDescSource: Folders and tags for the whole database.

quickSwitcher.placeholder: Type to search or switch file...

imageViewer.viewOnly: View only – editable formats are PNG, JPG and WebP.

okfInfo.title: What is OKF?
okfInfo.intro: OKF (Open Knowledge Format) is an open convention for Markdown knowledge collections: plain Markdown files with a small, uniform frontmatter header. Your vault stays fully portable.
okfInfo.whatBody: Every newly created note gets two frontmatter fields: “type” (what kind of document it is, e.g. Note or Daily Note – configurable per vault) and “okf_version” (the version of the convention). Existing files are never changed unasked; the optional conversion lives in the settings.
okfInfo.whyBody: Plainva builds on OKF because reliable structure is what makes plain files powerful: databases (.base), filters and templates can trust every note to carry a type, folders stay navigable through index.md, and scripts or AI assistants can work with your vault safely. And it is an open convention instead of a lock-in — plain Markdown that other OKF tools understand too, today and in ten years.
okfInfo.indexBody: An index.md is a folder's table of contents. Plainva generates it on request (right-click a folder) and keeps self-generated listings up to date automatically whenever the folder changes. Such files carry an invisible marker at the end and open read-only – “Edit anyway” hands them over to manual care at any time.
okfInfo.obsidianBody: Opening and editing an existing Obsidian vault in Plainva is safe — nothing is migrated or reformatted, and Obsidian keeps opening every file Plainva writes. One caveat in the other direction: once a vault uses Plainva features (database extensions such as boards, relations or reverse columns, managed index.md files), editing those files in Obsidian can break that functionality, because Obsidian does not know the plainva: extensions. Opening and reading is always safe.
okfInfo.settingsDesc: In short: type, okf_version and index.md management.

splash.title: Welcome to Plainva

splash.openLocalVaultDesc: Select a local folder on your computer.
splash.openWebDavVaultDesc: Connect to Nextcloud, ownCloud, or WebDAV.

splash.removeFromListHint: Removes it from Plainva only — the files stay on disk
splash.removeDialogBody: Remove "{{name}}" from the list? You can additionally forget all of this vault's app data (search index, settings, window layout, sync credentials) — reopening the folder then behaves like a brand-new vault. Your vault folder with the notes stays completely untouched either way.
splash.removeForgetPartial: Some app data for "{{name}}" could not be removed ({{what}}).

indexMd.title: index.md manager
indexMd.intro: Create OKF directory listings (index.md) per folder or adopt an existing overview note (MOC, Übersicht, …). Renames only happen on your explicit choice — links are updated vault-wide.
indexMd.statusConcept: index.md is a regular note (reserved name)
indexMd.prepareOption: Prepare adopted files for OKF (remove frontmatter, convert this file's wikilinks to markdown links)
indexMd.renameHint: An index.md used as a regular note can be renamed in the file tree — links are updated automatically.
indexMd.generateAllMissing: Generate index.md in all {{count}} folders without one
indexMd.resultPrepLeftovers: {{embeds}} embed(s) and {{unresolved}} unresolvable wikilinks left unchanged
indexMd.updateAllResult: {{updated}} index.md updated, {{skipped}} without managed marker skipped.
indexMd.managedBanner: This index.md is managed by Plainva and updated automatically.
indexMd.editAnywayConfirm: The managed marker will be removed; the file will no longer update automatically. Continue?
indexMd.overwriteConfirm: {{path}} already exists and will be regenerated (a backup is created). Continue?

versions.title: Version history
versions.empty: No saved versions yet. Snapshots are created automatically as you edit.
versions.orphanHint: This file no longer exists in the vault. Restoring recreates it at its original location.
versions.restoreConfirmMsg: Replace the current content of "{{name}}" with the version from {{when}}? The current state is saved as a snapshot first.
versions.binaryNoPreview: No preview for this file type.
versions.deletedHint: Every deletion in Plainva saves a snapshot first. Files removed by deleting a whole folder may only have older snapshots — the system trash is the primary recovery for those.

compat.title: Plainva extension
compat.bodySuffix: is a Plainva-specific extension. It is not fully supported natively by Obsidian (or the Obsidian Database Folder plugin) and may appear there as a table or list.

graph.pinModeOn: Positions are remembered — click to discard

graph.cleanupMentionGone: The occurrence no longer exists — rescan the list.
graph.edgeRemoveLinksMsg: Every text link from “{{source}}” to “{{target}}” will be replaced by its display text.
graph.edgeRemoveRelationMsg: “{{property}}” in “{{source}}” loses its entry pointing at “{{target}}”.
graph.baseNoRelations: This database has no relations — edges show wiki links between the entries.
graph.baseShowExternalTip: Also show notes a relation points at outside this view
graph.baseShowIncomingTip: Also show notes from other databases whose relation points at entries in this view (e.g. a project's tasks)
graph.baseColorByTip: Color nodes by the value of a select property
graph.baseSizeByTip: Scale node size by the value of a number property

backup.snapshotFailed: Version snapshot for {{path}} failed — the file was still saved. Check disk space and the backup folder.

reader.mermaidError: Mermaid diagram could not be rendered

conflict.title: Resolve sync conflict
conflict.explainer: '{{file}}' was changed on both sides. The left shows the file's current state (the other side), the right your preserved version — edit the right side and pull individual blocks over from the left.
conflict.keepOtherMsg: Your conflict copy will be deleted; the file keeps the other side's state. A version snapshot of the copy remains in the version history.
conflict.notAConflictFile: This file is not a conflict copy.

mobile.syncFolderAfterConnect: The cloud folder is chosen after connecting.
mobile.vaultNotConnected: This vault is no longer connected.
mobile.syncCreatesVaultHint: Connecting creates a new, separate vault for this cloud location.
mobile.moreArrangeHint: Drag the handle to arrange. The top three appear in the navigation bar below.
mobile.vaultCreateLocalDesc: Notes stay on this device — you can connect a cloud anytime.
mobile.vaultCreateOnlineDesc: Nextcloud/WebDAV, Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox or S3 — uploaded to the cloud.
mobile.onboardingCloudExistingDesc: Bring cloud content onto this device.
mobile.onboardingCloudNew: Create a new vault in the cloud
mobile.onboardingCloudNewDesc: Empty or with a starter structure — uploaded to the cloud.
mobile.vaultDeleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}" from this device? Local files, index and credentials are removed; the cloud storage stays untouched.
mobile.aboutTip: Tip: the logo likes attention. Five times.
mobile.photoInsertFailed: The photo could not be added: {{error}}
mobile.deleteNoteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}"? With sync active it is deleted in the cloud too.
mobile.onboardingBody: Your notes are plain Markdown files — private, portable, yours.
mobile.onboardingLocalDesc: Notes stay on this device. You can connect a cloud any time.
mobile.onboardingCloudDesc: Sync a vault from Nextcloud, Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox or S3 — existing or new.
mobile.deleteFolderConfirm: Delete the folder "{{name}}" and all notes in it?
mobile.noBacklinks: No other note links here yet.
mobile.conflictHint: Two versions of this note exist. Backups keep every version, so nothing is lost for good.
mobile.conflictPreserved: Conflict: your edits were saved as a conflict copy.
mobile.saveRetry: Saving failed — retrying in the background.
mobile.noteMissing: This note could not be found.
mobile.databasesEmpty: No databases yet. Create a .base file to get started.
mobile.baseGraphFallback: The graph view is available on desktop; shown here as a table.
mobile.todayNothing: Nothing was edited on this day.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.promoteNoDb: No standard task database set — pick one in Settings under “Content & structure”.
tasks.promoteNoFolder: The database has no storage folder — set one in the database's configure panel.

tags.renameMessage: New name for #{{tag}} (across the whole vault):

pinboard.sortActive: Sort rule active — manual arranging is disabled.

pim.openVaultFirst: Only available for the open vault.
pim.removeAccountMsg: “{{label}}” will be removed from this vault. Nothing is deleted at the provider.
pim.googleByoHint: Google requires your own OAuth client ID (like the Drive sync). Scopes: calendar + tasks.
pim.googleClientIdRequired: Google needs your own client ID (BYO).
pim.microsoftHint: Uses the central Plainva app registration — just connect and consent in the browser.
pim.calendarEmptyHint: Connect a CalDAV, Google or Microsoft account in Settings under “Calendar & accounts”.
pim.meetingNoteFailed: Could not create the meeting note.
pim.meetingFolderHint: Where notes from “Event → meeting note” in the calendar are stored.
pim.deleteEventMsg: “{{title}}” will be deleted from the provider's calendar.
pim.eventColorDesc: Overrides the calendar colour for this event
pim.eventConflict: The event changed remotely — view refreshed.
pim.seriesEditMsg: “{{title}}” is part of a series. What do you want to edit?
pim.seriesDeleteMsg: “{{title}}” is part of a series. What do you want to delete?
pim.attendeesChipHint: Enter an email address and press Enter
pim.blockHint: Mirror “{{title}}” into other calendars as a blocker.
pim.rsvpUnsupported: Responding isn't supported for this account.
pim.removeAccountConfirm: The credentials and cached events are removed (the calendar at the provider stays).
pim.connectCaldavHint: Connect CalDAV with an app password (e.g. Fastmail, Nextcloud, iCloud). Google/Microsoft follow via browser sign-in.

mail.title: Email
mail.emptyHint: Connect an IMAP account in Settings under “Calendar & accounts”.
mail.imapHint: Plainva reads without marking messages as read; flag, move and delete actions only run when you request them. Gmail: imap.gmail.com, port 993, with an app password.
mail.microsoftHint: For Outlook.com / Microsoft 365: sign in directly in the browser — no app password, no IMAP.
mail.folderHint: Where captured emails (notes and .eml files) are stored.
mail.bodyPlaceholder: Write your message… (Markdown, like the editor)
mail.copied: Formatted text copied — paste it into your mail program.
mail.mailtoTruncated: The text was shortened for mailto.
mail.saveDraft: Save note as email draft in the mailbox
mail.draftSaved: Draft stored in the mailbox — open your mail program to send it.
mail.draftHint: Plainva never sends itself — the draft lands in the mailbox and is sent from your mail program.
mail.loadRemoteImagesHint: Loading remote images lets the sender see your IP address and when you opened the mail (tracking). Plainva blocks them by default — use “Show images” to reveal them per message.
mail.composeHint: “Send” delivers directly via SMTP; “Save as draft” puts the message in your mailbox.
mail.noSmtpHint: Add an SMTP host to the account to send directly.
mail.deleteMsg: The message will be moved to Trash.
mail.deleteMsgN: {{n}} messages will be moved to the trash.
mail.gmailLabelsChanged: Changed the Gmail label for {{n}} message(s) to {{folder}}.
mail.gmailAllMailDeleteWarning: This removes the message globally from all Gmail labels and moves it to Trash.
mail.deleteForeverMsg: The message will be deleted permanently.

cloudAccounts.searchNoProvider: No provider found. Use the "WebDAV / CalDAV", "Object storage (S3)" or "Email server (IMAP)" tiles for any other service.
cloudAccounts.hintAppPassword: {{provider}} requires an app password — the regular account password is rejected.
cloudAccounts.hintAuthCode: {{provider}} requires an authorization code from the mailbox settings — not the account password.
cloudAccounts.hintMailPassword: {{provider}} requires a separate mail password, set independently of the account login.
cloudAccounts.hintEnableFirst: IMAP must first be enabled in the settings of {{provider}}.
cloudAccounts.hintBridge: Requires the locally running Proton Mail Bridge (a paid Proton feature).
cloudAccounts.presetUseMicrosoft: Microsoft turned off password sign-in for IMAP. Connect this mailbox through the Microsoft tile.
cloudAccounts.appleNoFiles: iCloud Drive offers no interface for other apps — files are not possible with Apple.
cloudAccounts.filesXorNote: Only one account per vault can carry Files — currently {{account}}.
cloudAccounts.servicesHint: The selection controls which permissions are requested.
cloudAccounts.svcMailDesc: Read, send and capture messages into the vault.
cloudAccounts.filesTakenDetail: Runs via {{account}}. To switch, disable it there first, then enable it here.
cloudAccounts.gmailHint: Gmail connects via an IMAP app password — no Google verification review. Create the app password in your Google account under “Security”.
cloudAccounts.sequenceHint: Each service may need its own browser sign-in — the status walks you through.
cloudAccounts.calendarNext: Pick calendars and task lists in the “Calendar” area.
cloudAccounts.removeAccountMsg: All services of this account are removed from this vault. Nothing is deleted at the provider.
cloudAccounts.byoAccountHint: By default Plainva uses its central app registration. An own app id applies to the whole account.
cloudAccounts.byoAccountHintGoogle: Google requires your own OAuth client ID (like the Drive sync). It applies to the whole account.
cloudAccounts.serviceOffMsg: “{{service}}” is turned off for this account and the service's local data is removed. Nothing is deleted at the provider.
cloudAccounts.svcConfigureSync: Configure folder and behavior in the “Sync” area.
cloudAccounts.svcConfigureMail: Configure capture and privacy in the “Email” area.
cloudAccounts.useWizardHint: Add this service via “Connect account…” with the same provider — it is grouped into this account automatically.
cloudAccounts.noServiceMailBody: Enable Email on a cloud account to see your mailbox here.
cloudAccounts.noServiceCalBody: Enable Calendar & tasks on a cloud account to see events here.

workspaceSecurity.qrScanning: Point the camera at the QR code
workspaceSecurity.qrCameraFailed: Camera unavailable — paste the code instead.
workspaceSecurity.liftEncryptionDesc: Turns this vault back into a normal, unencrypted cloud vault: your notes are uploaded to the same cloud as plain files.
workspaceSecurity.liftEncryptionConfirm: Your notes will be uploaded to the cloud as plain, unencrypted files, and this vault stops being encrypted. Local files are not changed and nothing is deleted. The old encrypted copy stays in the cloud until you delete that folder yourself.
workspaceSecurity.liftEncryptionDone: Encryption lifted. Your notes are uploading to the cloud as plain files. Delete the old encrypted .pvws/ folder at your provider once the upload has finished.
workspaceSecurity.inviteQrCaption: Or scan this code with the Plainva app on your other device
workspaceSecurity.pairingQrCaption: The approver can scan this code instead of typing it.
workspaceSecurity.manageAccessDesc: Pair another device · save the recovery package
workspaceSecurity.cloudDisconnect: Remove the connection to the encrypted cloud
workspaceSecurity.cloudDisconnectDesc: Removes only this device. The cloud stays encrypted — other devices and members keep access.
workspaceSecurity.addDeviceDesc: Pairs a second device of your own.
workspaceSecurity.addDeviceHint: Paste this code on your other device (Security & Sharing → join), then approve it here. Your other devices stay.
workspaceSecurity.workspaceProtected: Content is stored remotely as encrypted objects with signed changes.
workspaceSecurity.connectionRequired: Connect this vault to a cloud provider before enabling encrypted sync.
workspaceSecurity.openVaultFirst: Open this vault to manage its security settings.
workspaceSecurity.vaultOpenedContinue: Vault opened. Choose the action again to continue.
workspaceSecurity.connectionOpened: Connect a file provider, then return to Security & Sharing.
workspaceSecurity.revokeFullQuestion: Access is removed immediately and all current encrypted content is queued for a resumable full rekey. Previously downloaded plaintext cannot be taken back.
workspaceSecurity.publicationIsolation: Published slices use a separate encrypted workspace namespace. Provider permissions add defense in depth; they never replace encryption.
workspaceSecurity.publishDesc: Choose exact or sanitized content, read/comment/suggestion access and a provider.
workspaceSecurity.providerAclHint: Provider permissions are defense in depth. Encryption remains authoritative.
workspaceSecurity.sliceDetailsHint: Use a name recipients will recognize.
workspaceSecurity.sliceReviewHint: Review the materialized scope. Sanitized projections scrub excluded links and private properties.
workspaceSecurity.unlockToApprove: Unlock an existing workspace device to approve this request.
workspaceSecurity.ownerTransferRequirements: Choose the new owner and current recovery file.
workspaceSecurity.ownerTransferDone: Ownership and recovery were transferred. Give file and code through separate secure channels.
workspaceSecurity.ownerTransferWarning: The new file is saved before anchor and owner policy activate. You become Admin; the target becomes the only Owner.
workspaceSecurity.revokeFutureQuestion: Future-only rotation is fast: access to new keys ends now, but encrypted history is not rewritten.
workspaceSecurity.lockedMessage: The encrypted workspace is locked on this device.

roleDesc.Owner: Full control, including ownership and recovery.

workspaceSecurity.membersDesc: People with encrypted access. Inviting reserves a place — pairing their device hands over the key.
workspaceSecurity.model1: The owner invites you — this reserves your place.
workspaceSecurity.model2: You send a join request; an existing device approves it — this hands over the key.
workspaceSecurity.model3: Your device becomes active and the vault decrypts.
workspaceSecurity.joinInviteHint: Paste the invitation code the workspace owner gave you.
workspaceSecurity.joinWaiting: Waiting for an existing device to approve this join. Keep Plainva open.
workspaceSecurity.joinShortCodeHint: Give this code to the approving device
workspaceSecurity.joinFailed: Join failed. Check the invitation code.
workspaceSecurity.joinMismatch: This invitation is for a different workspace than the one on this remote.
workspaceSecurity.joinDetected: This vault is protected by an encrypted workspace. If you were invited, join it here — pairing this device hands over the key.
workspaceSecurity.membersModel: Inviting reserves a place. The invited person opens Security & Sharing on their device, pastes the invitation code and requests to join; an existing device approves it, which hands over the key.
workspaceSecurity.inviteArtifactHint: Send this code to the invited person through a secure channel. On their device they open Security & Sharing, paste it and request to join; approving their device here hands over the key.
workspaceSecurity.restoreVsJoin: Restore is a last resort: it creates a new owner device and revokes all other devices. To add a second or returning device normally, use the join flow — it keeps the other devices.
workspaceSecurity.nativeKeychain: Keys are protected by the operating-system keychain.
workspaceSecurity.passphraseProtected: Keys are protected by your local passphrase.
workspaceSecurity.unlockFailed: The workspace could not be unlocked. Check the passphrase.
workspaceSecurity.recoverySetupHint: A recovery package is created during setup.
workspaceSecurity.recoverySeparation: Keep the .pvrecovery file and recovery code in separate safe places. Neither part contains cloud credentials.
workspaceSecurity.devicesP4: Adding, pairing, and revoking further devices follows in P4.
workspaceSecurity.teamsP4: Roles, groups, invitations, and shared slices follow in P4 and later phases.
workspaceSecurity.cleanupWarning: {{n}} legacy plaintext object(s) still exist next to the encrypted workspace at the provider.
workspaceSecurity.cleanupDesc: Local plaintext files remain untouched. Only the old provider-side copy is removed.
workspaceSecurity.cleanupBody: This permanently deletes {{n}} legacy plaintext object(s) from the cloud provider. The encrypted .pvws copy and local files remain.
workspaceSecurity.cleanupFailed: The plaintext cleanup could not be completed.
workspaceSecurity.joinHelp: On the inviting device open Security & Sharing, go to the team members, choose "Show invitation" and copy the code. Paste it here.
workspaceSecurity.joinPending: Share this code with the approver, then wait for approval.
workspaceSecurity.inviteInvalid: That is not a valid invitation code. Copy it from Security & Sharing on the inviting device.
workspaceSecurity.inviteMismatch: This invitation is for a different workspace than the one synced here.
workspaceSecurity.decommissionDesc: Remove the encrypted workspace from this device. Do this BEFORE deleting the vault in the cloud.
workspaceSecurity.decommissionConfirm: Plainva will remove the encrypted workspace from this device: the local keys and workspace data are cleared and the vault reopens as a normal vault. Encrypted files already in the cloud are not deleted — remove the cloud folder yourself afterwards. Without the recovery package this cannot be undone on this device.
workspaceSecurity.decommissionNote: This clears the local keys and workspace data and reopens the vault as a normal vault. Encrypted files already in the cloud are not deleted — remove the cloud folder yourself afterwards.
workspaceSecurity.orphanRecovery: If the encrypted workspace was deleted or damaged in the cloud, sync stays stopped to protect your data. Decommission the workspace on this device below to reset it.
workspaceSecurity.setupIntro: Plainva creates a personal owner identity and device keys. Your local vault stays readable; cloud content moves to encrypted objects.
workspaceSecurity.setupFailed: The encrypted workspace could not be prepared.
workspaceSecurity.recoverySetupIntro: You will need both the file and the code if every device is lost. Complete these three tasks before encryption starts.
workspaceSecurity.recoveryTaskFileDesc: Keep it somewhere you can reach if this device is lost.
workspaceSecurity.recoveryTaskCodeDesc: Use a password manager, printed copy, or another safe location — not beside the recovery file.
workspaceSecurity.recoveryTaskCheckTitle: Check that you can read the code
workspaceSecurity.recoveryTaskCheckDesc: Enter groups {{first}} and {{second}} from the highlighted list above.
workspaceSecurity.recoveryCopyFailed: The recovery code could not be copied.
workspaceSecurity.recoveryNextCheck: Next: enter both highlighted code groups.
workspaceSecurity.recoveryReady: Recovery backup checked. You can activate encryption.
workspaceSecurity.recoveryWarning: Save the file and code separately. Losing both means losing recovery access.
workspaceSecurity.recoveryFileDesc: Encrypted package with recovery and owner keys; contains no cloud credentials.
workspaceSecurity.saveFailed: The recovery file could not be saved.
workspaceSecurity.confirmRecovery: I saved the recovery file and code in separate safe places.
workspaceSecurity.activating: Publishing the workspace and preparing the resumable encryption queue…
workspaceSecurity.activationFailed: Activation failed. The existing remote plaintext was not removed.
workspaceSecurity.pairHelp: Enter the request and compare its fingerprint before approval.
workspaceSecurity.compareFingerprint: Approve only if this fingerprint exactly matches the new device.
workspaceSecurity.revokeDeviceDesc: The device immediately loses future sync access.
workspaceSecurity.memberIdRequired: Enter the member ID created by an administrator.
workspaceSecurity.qrUnsupported: QR recognition is unavailable; use the manual code.
workspaceSecurity.noRemoteWorkspace: No encrypted workspace was found on this connection.
workspaceSecurity.restoreDesc: Use the recovery file and separate code if every device is unavailable.
workspaceSecurity.restoreWarning: Recovery creates a new owner device and revokes lost devices without rewriting content.
workspaceSecurity.rotateRecoveryDesc: Replace and anchor the recovery identity with a new two-piece recovery set.
workspaceSecurity.rotateRecoveryWarning: The current file and code are required. A new file and separate code replace them.
workspaceSecurity.rotateRecoveryDone: The renewed file is saved and anchored. Store the new code separately; the old set is invalid.
workspaceSecurity.saveRequired: Save the renewed recovery file to finish.
workspaceSecurity.storeCodeSeparately: Store this code separately from the renewed file.
workspaceSecurity.memberInvited: Member created; pair their device next.
workspaceSecurity.revokeMemberDesc: Their devices are revoked and affected keys rotate. Previously downloaded content cannot be taken back.
workspaceSecurity.groupsDesc: Encryption groups and their effective role.
workspaceSecurity.slicesDesc: Folder, explicit selection, or dynamic rule.
workspaceSecurity.slicePreview: Preview is calculated locally before the signed policy is published.
workspaceSecurity.name: Name
workspaceSecurity.moveAccessLossMessage: After moving this item to {{path}}, you may no longer be able to read it. Continue?
workspaceSecurity.readOnly: Read-only access — changes cannot be saved.
workspaceSecurity.commentOnly: Comment-only access — file content is read-only.

cascade.primaryNoteDesc: The note itself. A version snapshot is kept.
cascade.primaryBaseDesc: The database view (configuration file). Without the checkboxes below, every note stays.
cascade.assignedDesc: Elements whose properties point at deleted items — including their sub-elements. Shared elements (also assigned elsewhere) are excluded.
cascade.dbItemsDesc: Notes from the data source. Folder overviews (index.md) and attachments stay.
cascade.linkedAssignedDesc_one: {{count}} element is assigned to the deleted items.
cascade.linkedAssignedDesc_other: {{count}} elements are assigned to the deleted items.
cascade.linkedAllDesc: Deletes the database file and ALL of its elements — including those without an assignment.
cascade.excludedShared_one: {{count}} shared element excluded — it is also assigned elsewhere.
cascade.excludedShared_other: {{count}} shared elements excluded — they are also assigned elsewhere.
cascade.excludedMembers_one: {{count}} element excluded: it is also a row of another database.
cascade.excludedMembers_other: {{count}} elements excluded: they are also rows of other databases.
cascade.cleanupDesc_one: Removes references to deleted items from the properties of {{count}} remaining note. Body links stay untouched.
cascade.cleanupDesc_other: Removes references to deleted items from the properties of {{count}} remaining notes. Body links stay untouched.
cascade.cleanupTaskDb: The “Default task database” setting will be reset. Tasks at Google/Microsoft remain untouched.
cascade.cleanupTemplates: Template assignments to this database will be removed.
cascade.footHint: Version snapshots are kept — recoverable via “{{restore}}”.

whatsNew.title: What's New in Plainva
whatsNew.subtitle: Release Highlights
whatsNew.highlight1: PKM Import Engine: Import notes directly from Notion, Evernote, Google Keep, Logseq & Simplenote
whatsNew.highlight2: Design Language 2.0: 100% Token strictness & 14 full themes (incl. LCARS & Win95)
whatsNew.highlight3: Live QR scan & Mobile App optimizations
